news.summary: Investview, Inc., headquartered in Haverford, PA, released its Q1 2025 financial results today. The company, trading on OTCQB under the symbol INVU, is a financial technology services provider with various divisions. These include a financial education division that offers tools and content through a global network of independent distributors, a manufacturing division producing health, nutrition, and cognitive wellness products for wholesale and retail markets, and an online trading platform planning to provide retail brokerage services. Additionally, Investview owns a sustainable blockchain business specializing in bitcoin mining. The report highlighted operational achievements and strategic milestones as part of the company's global expansion plans.
news.headline: Investview, Inc. reported Q1 2025 financial results, offering financial tech services with a global network.
Planet 13 reveals Q1 2025 financial performance.

news.summary: Gold mining stocks, top performers in 2025, saw a significant decline after investor interest in safe havens waned due to the U.S.-China trade truce. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF (NYSE: GDX) dropped 9.6% over three sessions, its worst performance since April 2022. Gold prices also fell by 4.4%, currently 9.2% below the all-time high of $3,500 per ounce on April 22. Losses were widespread among GDX components, with Newmont Corp. (NYSE: NEM) falling 10% in three days, along with Barrick Gold Corp. (NYSE: GOLD) and Agnico Eagle Mines Ltd. (NYSE: AEM). This reversal occurred as global risk appetite increased, reducing the appeal of gold. The announcement of a 90-day U.S.-China tariff rollback on May 12 boosted equities and risk assets, leading investors to decrease their gold hedges. Technical analysts are advising caution for gold investments. Bank of America technical analyst, Paul Ciana, highlighted concerning daily gold technical setups.
news.headline: Gold miners' stocks hit hard after US-China trade truce, VanEck Gold Miners ETF plunged 9.6% over three sessions. Gold prices dropped by 4.4%. Newmont Corp. (NEM) dropped 10%. Global risk appetite surged post May 12 tariff rollback announcement. Analysts signal caution in gold sector.

news.summary: Arcosa, Inc. (NYSE: ACA), a company specializing in infrastructure products, has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.05 per share on its $0.01 par value common stock. This dividend will be paid on July 31, 2025, to stockholders of record as of July 15, 2025. Arcosa is headquartered in Dallas, Texas, and aims to provide infrastructure-related solutions.
news.headline: Arcosa, Inc. to pay $0.05/share dividend on July 31, 2025 to stockholders of record as of July 15, 2025.

news.summary: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong revealed that around 60 cryptocurrency founders gathered in Washington, DC, to meet with lawmakers regarding the GENIUS Act and a market structure bill. The Senate might vote on the GENIUS Act soon after it initially failed on May 8. Democrats are hesitant to support crypto legislation without safeguards against President Trump profiting from his digital asset ventures. Cointelegraph reached out to Coinbase for comment but did not receive a response. The situation is evolving, and more details will be provided as they emerge.
news.headline: Crypto industry leaders convene in DC to back Senate stablecoin bill; up to 60 founders urge support for GENIUS Act after initial failed vote. Senate may re-vote soon on bill after Dems' concerns on Trump's digital asset ventures. More details to follow.

news.summary: Xylem Inc. (NYSE: XYL) has announced a second quarter dividend of $0.40 per share. The dividend will be paid on June 26, 2025, to shareholders of record as of May 29, 2025. Xylem, a Fortune 500 company, is a global leader in water solutions, aiming to create a more water-secure world. With approximately 23,000 employees, the company generated $8.6 billion in revenue in 2024, focusing on innovative water and resource management. #LetsSolveWater.
news.headline: Xylem Inc. (NYSE: XYL) declares 2nd quarter dividend of $0.40/share, payable on June 26, 2025, for shareholders of record by May 29.

(NASDAQ: VGAS) released its first quarter 2025 results, highlighting progress in deploying liquid fuels processing technology through commercial production plants. The quarter saw a significant $50 million equity investment by Cottonmouth in Verde. Additionally, the company made strides in advancing Front End Engineering Design (FEED) for the Permian Basin project, focusing on a natural gas-to-gasoline plant. These developments showcase Verde's commitment to growth and innovation in the energy sector.

news.summary: In Miami, I Squared Capital, a top global infrastructure investment manager, and The Arab Energy Fund (TAEF) – previously APICORP, a key impact financial institution in the MENA energy sector – have agreed to a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding (MoU). Their partnership will involve investing in a specialized infrastructure investment plan concentrated on the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. This strategy aims to boost connectivity within and beyond the MENA region.
news.headline: I Squared Capital & TAEF sign MoU to invest in MENA energy sector with new infrastructure strategy.

news.summary: Ooma, Inc. (NYSE: OOMA), a company offering advanced communication services, will be participating in the 45th Annual William Blair Growth Stock Conference. The event will take place in Chicago, Illinois, with Ooma's CEO Eric Stang and CFO Shig Hamamatsu presenting from 4:40 p.m. to 5:10 p.m. Central time on June 4, 2025, along with various investor meetings scheduled throughout the day. This conference serves as a platform for Ooma to showcase its business to potential investors and partners.

news.summary: In Ashburn, Virginia, DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC) announced its financial results for Q4 and FY 2025. CEO Raul Fernandez stated that the Q4 results show advancement towards their target of consistent, profitable revenue growth. They saw over 20% growth in bookings for the second quarter in a row, with book-to-bill ratios surpassing 1. Despite the uncertain macro environment, DXC is maintaining its focus on growth.
news.headline: DXC Tech (NYSE: DXC) announced Q4 results with bookings growth>20% and book to bill ratios>1, aiming for sustained, profitable revenue.

news.summary: ASHBURN, Va.--DXC Technology disclosed that the Board of Directors has given the green light to special equity grants for Raul Fernandez, the President and CEO, and Rob Del Bene, the Executive Vice President and CFO. These grants aim to ensure the ongoing leadership of the two executives until fiscal year 2028 and enhance their compensation to better reflect long-term shareholder value. The special equity grants were approved unanimously by the Board.
news.headline: DXC Tech extends CEO and CFO agreements to foster growth and shareholder value. Special equity grants secure executives through 2028.

news.summary: Adobe (stock symbol: ADBE) based in San Jose, California, has appointed Louise Pentland as the Chief Legal Officer and Executive Vice President. Pentland, who will report to Chair and CEO Shantanu Narayen, will head the Legal and Government Relations team, managing worldwide legal and policy issues. With a strong background in the technology sector, she has previously served as chief legal officer at various multinational firms. Pentland's extensive experience in corporate law makes her a valuable addition to Adobe's leadership team.
news.headline: Adobe appoints Louise Pentland as Chief Legal Officer and Executive VP, who will oversee legal matters globally.

news.summary: Rocket Lab USA, Inc., a prominent company in the aerospace industry listed on Nasdaq as RKLB, has recently disclosed that it will be overseeing the launch of NASA's Aspera mission. This mission, focused on astrophysics research, aims to delve into the intricacies of galaxy development and evolution, advancing our understanding of the universe. The Aspera spacecraft is set to be launched by Rocket Lab's Electron from Launch Complex 1, with the earliest launch window scheduled for the first quarter of 2026. This collaboration signifies a significant step forward in space exploration and scientific discovery.
news.headline: Rocket Lab chosen by NASA to launch Aspera mission to study galaxy evolution on Electron rocket in Q1 2026. $RKLB

news.summary: Colvin has been announced as the successor to the retiring CEO Bonnie Kintzer at Trusted Media Brands in New York as of May 14, 2025. The company is known for hosting popular brands including Taste of Home, The Pet Collective, Family Handyman, FailArmy, The Healthy, and Reader's Digest. This change comes after Kintzer's long-standing tenure as the CEO.
news.headline: Trusted Media Brands names Stephen Colvin as new CEO, taking over from retiring Bonnie Kintzer.

news.summary: In Bristol, Pennsylvania on May 14, 2025, TESCO Metering celebrates becoming one of the initial manufacturers to be certified under the National Electrical Manufacturers Association's (NEMA) fresh Make It American™ initiative. This recognition highlights TESCO's commitment to domestic manufacturing. They join as a pilot partner in the program, showcasing their dedication to promoting American-made products.
news.headline: TESCO Metering recognized as pilot partner in NEMA's Make It American™ Program, emphasizing US manufacturing.

news.summary: Contango ORE, Inc. announced their Q1-2025 financial results, revealing total income of $19.3 million compared to a $2.9 million loss in Q1-2024. They reported a net loss of $22.3 million for Q1-2025, inclusive of a $40.5 million non-cash expense from derivative contracts. Gold sales in Q1-2025 amounted to 17,382 ounces with Cash Costs of $1,334 per ounce and AISC of $1,374 per ounce. Their unrestricted cash position as of March 31, 2025, was $35.0 million, up from $20.1 million in December 2024. The Company's Q1-2025 net cash from operating activities was $28.6 million, a notable improvement from a $4.4 million use in Q1-2024. They experienced zero cash used in investing activities in Q1-2025, a change from $15.5 million in Q1-2024. Cash used in financing activities was $13.7 million in Q1-2025.Contango mentioned significant progress in various projects, including the successful production of gold exceeding quarterly guidance, reducing hedge balance, and initiation of processing in the Manh Choh second campaign. The Company received cash distributions of $24 million in Q1-2025 and an additional $9 million in April 2025. They also reported the completion of a Technical Report Summary on the Johnson Tract Project, showcasing robust economics. The Company aims to focus on permitting related to this project.
news.headline: Contango made $19.3M in Q1-2025, $22.3M net loss due to $40.5M unrealized loss on derivative contracts. Sold 17,382 ounces of gold with cash costs per ounce of $1,334. Unrestricted cash position at $35M on March 31, 2025. Company received $24M in cash distributions in Q1-2025. Placed 15,000 ounces production in Contango's account for the second campaign of 2025. Commission dismissed lawsuit against the State of Alaska DOT, enhancing impacts for Manh Choh project. NPV over $400M for Johnson Tract Project. Net cash provided from operating activities up at $28.6M in Q1-2025. Statement of Loss was $22.3M in Q1-2025.

news.summary: Edesa Biotech, Inc. reported its financial results for the three and six months ending March 31, 2025. The company completed a $15 million equity financing, focusing on the development of their vitiligo drug candidate EB06. Total operating expenses decreased to $1.6 million for the three months and $3.5 million for the six months. The company had cash and cash equivalents of $13.9 million and a working capital of $13.5 million. Edesa plans to submit drug manufacturing data to the FDA in the second half of 2025, with anticipated topline results in 12-18 months. The company is focused on developing treatments for immune-related diseases and has upcoming clinical studies for vitiligo and pulmonary fibrosis. The CEO highlighted the potential impact of their drug on vitiligo patients worldwide. The company aims to transform treatment paradigms through innovative immunotherapies. Financial results showed a net loss of $1.6 million for the quarter ending March 31, 2025, and $3.2 million for the six months. Edesa Biotech anticipates a positive future despite known and unknown risks associated with product development and commercialization. They will be participating in the BIO International Convention in June 2025 for networking and business opportunities.
news.headline: Edesa Biotech reports $15M equity financing, $13.9M cash on hand, focusing on vitiligo drug development. Net loss $3.2M in 6 months.

news.summary: Stellus Private Credit BDC reported strong financial results for the fourth fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2025. The company generated $0.31 per share of net investment income and maintained a stable net asset value. They funded $37 million of investments and received $4 million in repayments, bringing the total portfolio to $335 million at fair value. For the quarter, investments at fair value totaled $334.7 million, with total assets at $339.5 million. The weighted average yield of debt and other income-producing investments was 10.6%, and the weighted average yield on total investments was 10.0%. Investment income for the quarter was $8.1 million, with gross operating expenses at $5.3 million. The company's net investment income was $3.3 million, resulting in a net asset value of $165.48 per share. The company had $121.8 million in outstanding borrowings under the Credit Facility and $50.0 million under the SPV Facility. Distributions for the quarter totaled $0.36 per share. Stellus Private Credit BDC made several new investments in various companies during the period, showcasing its investment activity and portfolio diversification strategy.
news.headline: Stellus Private Credit BDC reports $0.31 per share net investment income, $37M funded investments, $335M total portfolio.

news.summary: In Torrance, California on May 14, 2025, Navitas Semiconductor (Nasdaq: NVTS) made public that Cristiano Amoruso is joining their board of directors right away. This addition to their board is a significant move for the company as it continues to expand its leadership team in the industry.
news.headline: Navitas Semiconductor appoints Cristiano Amoruso to board of directors. Cristiano to join NVTS board effective immediately.

news.summary: In Perrysburg, Ohio, on May 14, 2025, O-I Glass, Inc. (NYSE: OI) announced that Alan J. Murray is retiring from the company's Board of Directors post the Annual Meeting of Share Owners. Murray, a long-serving member of the Board, is stepping down after years of service. Additionally, at the same meeting, 11 director nominees were elected for one-year terms. Among the newly elected members are Eugenio Garza y Garza and Iain J. Mackay. The other directors elected are Samuel R. Chapin, David V. Clark, II, Eric J. Foss, Gordon J. Hardie, John Humphrey, Hari N. Nair, Cheri Phyfer, Catherine I. Slater, and Carol A. Williams.
news.headline: Alan J. Murray retiring from O-I board; Eugenio Garza y Garza and Iain J. Mackay elected. 11 directors elected.

news.summary: Nextracker, a prominent solar technology platform provider listed on Nasdaq under the symbol NXT, recently disclosed its financial performance for the fourth quarter and complete fiscal year 2025, concluding on March 31, 2025. In Q4 FY25, the company reported revenue of $924 million, marking an increase from $737 million in Q4 FY24 and up from $679 million in Q3 FY25. The GAAP Gross Profit stood at $306 million in Q4 FY25, compared to $340 million in Q4 FY24 and $241 million in Q3 FY25, with the GAAP Gross Margin reported at 33.1%, sliding from 46.2% in Q4 FY24 and 35.5% in Q3 FY25. On the bottom line, Nextracker recorded GAAP Net Income of $158 million in Q4 FY25, down from $223 million in Q4 FY24 and up from $117 million in Q3 FY25, with GAAP Net Income Margin at 17.1% in Q4 FY25, dropping from 30.3% in Q4 FY24 and matching the 17.3% in Q3 FY25. The GAAP Diluted EPS was $1.05 in Q4 FY25, compared to $0.79 in Q3 FY25.
news.headline: Nextracker (Nasdaq: NXT) reports Q4 FY25 revenue of $924 million with 33.1% gross margin and $158 million net income.

news.summary: In Fremont, California, Nextracker (Nasdaq: NXT) revealed its acquisition of Bentek Corporation, a U.S.-based company known for creating electrical infrastructure for various solar power plants. The acquisition, valued at about $78 million in cash with potential additional earnout considerations, brings together Bentek's pre-assembled eBOS solutions with Nextracker's renowned solar tracker platform. This move is expected to enhance Nextracker's position in the solar technology industry.
news.headline: Nextracker buys Bentek Corporation for $78 million, expands solar technology portfolio with eBOS solutions.

news.summary: Rich Okoney recently joined Cushman & Wakefield as the Global Data Center Practice Lead within the Integrated Facility Management Service Line team in Chicago. news.summary: Denver-based Spruce Power Holding Corporation, a key player in the distributed solar energy sector in the U.S., disclosed its financial performance for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. The quarter showcased notable achievements: revenues soared to $23.8 million, marking a 30% increase from the previous year; a net loss attributable to stockholders stood at $15.3 million; and operating EBITDA climbed to $12.3 million, representing a 15% rise year-over-year. These figures reflect the company's strong performance and growth trajectory in the renewable energy market.
news.headline: Spruce Power reported Q1 2025 results: $23.8M in revenues (up 30%), net loss of $15.3M, operating EBITDA of $12.3M (up 15%).

news.summary: In Tampa, Florida, Slide Insurance has confirmed pricing for its latest catastrophe bond, Purple Re Ltd. (Series 2025-1), valued at $250 million. This marks Slide's largest cat bond offering to date, surpassing the previous record by $40 million. The Series 2025-1 bond consists of two fully collateralized classes: $125 million for Class A notes priced at 7.25% and $125 million for Class B notes priced at 7.75%. This offering demonstrates continued growth and investment in the insurance-linked securities market.
news.headline: Slide Insurance secures $250M cat bond with Purple Re Ltd. 2025-1, its largest offering to date, exceeding previous by $40M.

news.summary: Worksport (NASDAQ: WKSP) is about to release its quarterly earnings report on May 15, 2025. Analysts predict an EPS of -$1.05, with investors eager for possible results surpassing estimates and positive guidance for the next quarter. The company beat EPS estimates last quarter, resulting in a 3.88% stock price increase. In Q4 2024, Worksport's EPS estimate was -$1.4 but reported -$1.14, leading to a 4.0% stock price increase. As of May 13, Worksport's stock traded at $3.48, down 41.92% over the last 52 weeks. Analysts have given Worksport a consensus Buy rating. This article provides insights for investors leading up to Worksport's earnings release.
news.headline: Worksport (WKSP) to report EPS of $-1.05 on 2025-05-15. Previous beat in EPS resulted in 3.88% share price increase. Stock at $3.48. Analyst consensus for Worksport is Buy.
